entries.definition:
(88:21) فَذَكِّرْ إِنَّمَا أَنتَ مُذَكِّرٌ so warn [them], you are only a warner.
ذَاكِرُون dhākirūn [pl. of act. part. ذَاكِر dhākir, fem. pl. ذَاكِرَات dhākirāt] those who are mindful, those who remember, those who recite (33:35) وَالذَّاكِرِينَ اللَّهَ كَثِيرًا وَالذَّاكِرَاتِ and those men and women who remember God often.

ذَكَر dhakar [n., dual ذَكَرَان dhakarān, pl. ذُكُور dhukūr and ذُكْرَان dhukrān] male (16:97) مَنْ عَمِلَ صَالِحًا مِّن ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنثَىٰ whosoever does right, whether male or female.

ادَّكَرَ ʾiddakara [v. VIII, assimilated form of اِذْتَكَرَ ʾidhtakara, intrans.] to remember, to recollect (12:45) وَقَالَ الَّذِي نَجَا مِنْهُمَا وَادَّكَرَ بَعْدَ أُمَّةٍ and said the one who had gained freedom of the two and remembered [Joseph] after a while.

مُدَّكِر muddakir [act. part. assimilated form of مُذْتَكِر mudhtakir] one who learns a lesson, one who is admonished, takes heed (54:15) وَلَقَدْ تَّرَكْنَاهَا ءَايَةً فَهَلْ مِن مُّدَّكِرٍ We have left it [the ark] as a sign; so, is there anyone who takes heed?

entries.definition:
dh–k–w the sun, (of fire) to burn strongly; intelligence; to be witty; to slaughter an animal in the ritually approved manner; to purify. Of this root, only ذَكَّيْتُمْ dhakkaytum occurs once in the Qur’an.
ذَكَّى dhakkā [v. II, trans.] [jur.] to slaughter in accordance with the teachings (5:3) حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْكُمُ الْمَيْتَةُ [...] وَمَا أَكَلَ السَّبُعُ إِلَّا مَا ذَكَّيْتُمْ forbidden to you [for food] un-slaughtered [lit. dead] animals [...] and what has been savaged by a beast of prey, save that which you [yourselves] have [managed to] slaughter [while it was still alive].
entries.definition_english:
dh–k–w the sun, (of fire) to burn strongly; intelligence; to be witty; to slaughter an animal in the ritually approved manner; to purify. Of this root, only ذَكَّيْتُمْ dhakkaytum occurs once in the Qur’an.
ذَكَّى dhakkā [v. II, trans.] [jur.] to slaughter in accordance with the teachings (5:3) حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْكُمُ الْمَيْتَةُ [...] وَمَا أَكَلَ السَّبُعُ إِلَّا مَا ذَكَّيْتُمْ forbidden to you [for food] un-slaughtered [lit. dead] animals [...] and what has been savaged by a beast of prey, save that which you [yourselves] have [managed to] slaughter [while it was still alive].
